abstract.text: Orchestrates the selfie capture flow - navigates between instructions, requesting permissions,
showing camera view, and displaying processing screen
Displays instructions to the user for how to take a good quality selfie
Encapsulates the bundle and tablename of the Localizable.strings file to be used within the SDK
Consent screen for SmileID
Responsible for showing the consent screen and the consent denied (try again) screens.
The  idTypeKey field is not populated/returned by the API response, hence it being marked as
mutable. However, it should be populated before usage of this class/when the response gets decoded
A dialog that shows a preview of the image captured by the camera and asks the user to confirm
that the image is of acceptable quality.
Country Code to ID Type (e.g. {“ZA”: [“NATIONAL_ID_NO_PHOTO”]}
Instructions for taking a good quality document photo. Optionally, allows user to select a photo from their gallery.
Asks user to confirm that they do actually want to deny consent
The Auth Smile request. Auth Smile serves multiple purposes:
This screen represents a generic Processing state. It has 3 sub-states: In Progress, Success, and
Error. These sub-states are represented by the [processingState] parameter.
This handles Instructions + Capture + Confirmation for a single side of a document
The countryCode field is not populated/returned by the API response, hence it being marked as
mutable. However, it should be populated before usage of this class/when the response gets decoded.
The same applies to availableIdTypes
The actual selfie capture screen, which shows the camera preview and the progress indicator
